items.speaker: Noisy Sound of Miners
items.text: Hey, I was almost frozen to death. I told you that thing underground is a ticking bomb.

items.speaker: Noisy Sound of Researchers
items.text: Sigh, the detectors are not responding from the bottom of the Mine. The data they recorded is probably lost. All those efforts in vain.

items.speaker: Noisy Sound of Researchers
items.text: Have you heard about it? The shutdown of the Mine wasn't a simple accident. It's because...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What are they talking about? Are they Lei'li and Modee?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I should go and ask them what happened to the Mine.

dialog.stepLabel: 传来通讯，召唤玩家上去
dialog.nodeDesc: 传来通讯，召唤玩家上去

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: The communication returns normal, and the temperature is rising too. {PlayerName}, looks like you succeeded.

items.speaker: Huayu
items.text: Uncle Hong is safely back too. The Mine is rebooting the Refinery Tower. Thank you for your efforts!

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Oh, you're back, {PlayerName}. Thank you for lifting us through this crisis.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Keep on calling me Uncle Hong.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Yes, with the help of this plate, which I found when I left the cave.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Chengwen told me in my dream that this kind of plate is made of a special mineral, and that words inscibed on it won't be worn away after years.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Look, {PlayerName}. He didn't lie. His name is inscribed on it in a perfect condition.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: It was all in my dream, but now it's here before our eyes.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: I only understand now that the dreams were real things.

options.text: You are calmer than I think.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: I don't know why, but it feels like I losing the ability to feel as I forget things. No matter it is sadness or regret.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: What is left was only scenarios. They keep overwhelming me until I lose breath.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: {PlayerName}, will you listen to the other half of my boring dream?

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: After Lampylumen Myriad was awakened, Chengwen and I walked the same path as we do today.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: We broke Ice Chrysalises, removed the block at the entrance, and managed to suppress Lampylumen Myriad.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: However, the Refinery Tower wasn't built in the dream. Suppressing Lampylumen Myriad could only buy time for the rescue from Jinzhou to arrive.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Chengwen was heavily injured in the combat, and he didn't make it on the way back to Jinzhou for healing.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Later, when the Ministry started to investigate the reason of the crisis at the Mine, I was ready for being held accountable.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: But what met me was actually applause and medals, which I only found strange.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: It was Chengwen, who took all the responsibilities himself on the way back to the Ministry.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: It was only out of his simple belief that I could rebuild the Mine, and that the Mine couldn't afford to lose two heads.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Was it guilt?

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: After that I started to study the documents Chengwen left. I picked up the piles of research materials that I myself pushed to the ground.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: And I found that we were both wrong. The coexistence wasn't a job to do at the two ends of a line, but one to do on the middle point.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: I found the initial ideas of the Refinery Tower from his notes, and it became what it is now after iterations.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: We managed the balance by suppressing Lampylumen Myriad's chill with the Refinery Tower's heat, so as to prevent it from awakening.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: It was with this report that Ministry of Development agreed on the proposal that the Mine coexists with the TD.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: This was the way of survival that he and I joined efforts to find, but his name was forever wiped from the Mine's documents, and marked as a sinner.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: And now, even I am losing his memories. I want to write something down, but my brain is just blank.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: I often go to the places he spent time at, but his traces are only disappearing, because the Mine is changing so fast.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Lucky I have some fragments that I can put down on the journal. These stories and arguments, and Jinzhou Stew that we both love.

options.text: So the journal is a recollection of the past.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Yes. What if one day I stop distinguishing the current from the past and forget myself?

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: What if I get swallowed in a woven world, just like today?

options.text: The past is irrelevant. You walked out of it.
The past is irrelevant. You faced everything.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Walked out... Maybe. I don't want to be a coward anymore.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Faced everything... Maybe. I don't want to be a coward anymore.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: And I don't want the kids to walk down my path again.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Maybe this crisis is a chance for growth for them. It will correct their overdependence on the experience of previous generations.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: The balance of our coexistence with TD is forever changing, and the Refinery Tower is only one part of it.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Interestingly, this is the first time I've seen Lei'li and Modee work together.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}, for listening to this boring dream until the end.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: You should be tired after all this, right? I already informed the food stall.

items.speaker: Uncle Hong
items.text: You must come and have a taste of the special Jinzhou Stew of the Mine.
